description: Fix panel session enforcer using log.Printf instead of structured logging, which is inconsistent with the rest of the codebase that uses internal/logger. Also the enforcer's Start() goroutine has no shutdown mechanism (no context cancellation).
steps: In panel/internal/sessions/enforcer.go: Replace the standard library 'log' import and log.Printf calls with structured JSON logging. The enforcer struct should accept a *slog.Logger or simply use the same pattern as the rest of the panel. Looking at panel/cmd/panel/main.go, the panel uses standard log.Println. So the enforcer is actually consistent. However, it lacks a shutdown mechanism. Add a context.Context or done channel to allow graceful shutdown of the background goroutine.
Modify the Enforcer struct to include a 'done chan struct{}' field and a Stop() method that closes the channel.
In the Start() method, use a select with the done channel and a time.After/ticker so the goroutine can be stopped gracefully.
Update panel/cmd/panel/main.go if needed to call enforcer.Stop() on shutdown (check if there's a graceful shutdown handler already).
acceptance_criteria: Enforcer has a Stop() method that signals the background goroutine to exit
The Start() goroutine respects the stop signal and exits cleanly
verification: Run: go build ./panel/... to confirm compilation
Run: go test ./panel/... to confirm tests pass
Run: go vet ./panel/... for static analysis
findings: Added done channel and Stop() method. Replaced infinite sleep loop with ticker+select pattern. Did NOT modify main.go since it has no graceful shutdown yet. go build, go test, and go vet all pass cleanly.
